title: Links, Images, References, Autolinks, and URL Safety · Behavior Case: Url Admission
summary: Nested brackets confuse destination parsing, unresolved references disappear, javascript URLs survive into output, duplicate labels change silently, and every image receives its filename as alt text. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the url admission increment in reference-aware accessible link and media model. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.
Normalize reference labels, detect duplicates, resolve forward references, preserve unresolved source, and report locations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-url-admission — Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-links-media-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-inline-links-media-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_inline_links_media_5(links: int = 9, resolved: int = 8, rejected: int = 1, unsafe_urls: int = 0, missing_alt_decisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(links, resolved, rejected) < 0:
        return False, links, "link-count"
    if resolved + rejected != links:
        return False, resolved, "link-reconciliation"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    if missing_alt_decisions > 0:
        return False, missing_alt_decisions, "media-alternative"
    assert resolved + rejected == links
    return True, resolved, "admitted-links-media"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-url-admission-1urwc2
# Competency: ssg-url-admission.
# Case 6173: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_url_admission_1urwc2(links: int = 9, resolved: int = 8, rejected: int = 1, unsafe_urls: int = 0, missing_alt_decisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1urwc2 = "1urwc2-2cwru1-6173"
    assert evidence_variant_1urwc2
    evidence_axes_1urwc2 = (
        "reader1urwc2",
        "source6173",
        "parse2cwru1",
        "route1urwc26173",
        "artifact61731urwc2",
        "recovery2cwru16173",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1urwc2)) == 6
    if min(links, resolved, rejected) < 0:
        return False, links, "link-count"
    if resolved + rejected != links:
        return False, resolved, "link-reconciliation"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    if missing_alt_decisions > 0:
        return False, missing_alt_decisions, "media-alternative"
    assert resolved + rejected == links
    return True, resolved, "admitted-links-media"

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding. Artifact under review: reference-aware accessible link and media model.

options.text: The Url Admission record says reference-aware accessible link and media model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect url admission evidence boundary for Url Admission.

For inspect url admission evidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Url Admission for reference-aware accessible link and media model.
The record names the assumption, applies Url Admission, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Url Admission model · Theory Url Admission
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Url Admission: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 3514 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Url Admission model · Theory Url Admission.
steps.why: Retrieving Url Admission through the concrete reference-aware accessible link and media model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Url Admission model · Theory Url Admission, keep Url Admission connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Url Admission model · Theory Url Admission, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Url Admission precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Url Admission model · Theory Url Admission, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 3514 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to resolve references and media through URL safety, integrity, and alternative-text decisions; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify relative, root-relative, fragment, mail, HTTP, data, and forbidden schemes before context-aware encoding.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Url Admission model · Theory Url Admission, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Percent-encoding an untrusted destination makes every URL safe.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-url-admission-1urwc2(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_url_admission_1urwc2\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(links, resolved, rejected\) < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?resolved \+ rejected != links)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafe_urls > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?missing_alt_decisions > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
